**Subject: Re: Using Your Visa Debit Card in South Korea**  Thank you for reaching out, and how exciting that you have an upcoming trip to South Korea! These are great questions to sort out before you travel.  Here's what we can share based on Visa's general guidelines:  **Will your card work there?** Yes, Visa debit cards work at millions of international merchants and ATMs worldwide, so you should be able to use your card in South Korea at locations that accept Visa.  **Should you notify your bank?** **Yes, absolutely.** We strongly recommend informing your bank before you travel. Without prior notice, international transactions can be flagged as suspicious activity and your card may be temporarily blocked — which is the last thing you'd want while abroad.  **What foreign transaction fees should you expect?** For international ATM withdrawals and purchases, you may encounter: - **Foreign transaction fees** charged by your card issuer - **Out-of-network ATM fees** from your bank - **Surcharge fees** from the ATM operator  However, please note that these fees are set by your card issuer and ATM operators — **not by Visa directly.** The exact amounts will vary depending on your specific card and bank.  **We recommend contacting your bank directly** before your trip to: 1. Notify them of your travel dates and destination 2. Confirm your card will work internationally 3. Get a clear breakdown of any applicable fees  Have a wonderful trip! 🌏

**Subject: Re: Unauthorized Hotel Charge on Your Visa Card**  Thank you for reaching out, and I completely understand how frustrating it is to see an unexpected charge appear after you've already cancelled a reservation — especially when you have the confirmation to prove it.  **Here's what we recommend:**  Since this appears to be an incorrect charge from the merchant, you have two options to pursue:  1. **Contact the merchant directly** — Reach out to the hotel with your cancellation confirmation email as supporting evidence. This is often the fastest path to resolution, as refunds are processed by the merchant. Once processed, the credit should appear on your Visa statement within **3–5 business days**.  2. **Contact your card-issuing bank** — If the merchant is unresponsive or unwilling to resolve the issue, contact the bank that issued your Visa card to file a formal dispute. Your cancellation confirmation email will serve as valuable supporting documentation. Keep in mind that disputes are generally filed within **60–120 days** of the transaction date, so it's best to act promptly.  > **Important note:** Please contact your bank or the merchant directly — Visa itself does not process disputes or refunds on individual transactions.  We hope this gets resolved quickly for you. Don't hesitate to reach out if you have any further questions!
